item.post_title: Stardrops taste like your favorite thing! What do they taste like to you?

Harvesting them with an axe will give you between 15 and 21 regular quality melons.
If you don't want to harvest one or both of them they will remain indefinitely as a decoration, until such time as you decide to harvest them.

**Overall plan looks like so:**
* upgrade axe to copper ASAP, even before pickaxe
* use the chair to go into secret woods every day (and/or use hardwood stumps on Forest and 4-corners farm)
* gather hardwood and plant mahogany seeds on your farm with a bit extra spread to them with clear ground around them
* let the mahogany grow and start dropping additional mahogany seeds
You want 300+ hardwood going into summer and 10-20 mahogany trees growing. Level 5 forester instead of gatherer can be better here as well.
For wild tree seeds:
* upgrade axe to steel
  + cut down every tree in the forest, mountains
  + cut down non-pine trees on your farm
* collect pine cones dropped by pine tree on your farm
* early summer start full-clearing your farm and start planting a grid of wild trees (you want a lot of pines for mushroom logs)
* cut down any regrown trees before green rain, after green rain and later on as needed.
**Tree fertilizer** can be handy on mahogany trees, but when used on any tree it makes them grow moss quicker as well, so bonus points for using tree fertilizer on every pine tree you plant.
**Green rain** will hit somewhere in summer and your goal is to purely go for moss - farm every weed and moss in the forest and open areas of your farm and other zones. You should get bit over 300 moss or 400 if you are on beach farm and it's mostly cleared before rain.
300 moss then gives you 30 logs which can produce around 1050 mushrooms per season. After green rain moss harvesting will be slow, but the more trees you will have the more moss you will be able to farm regularly so a mushroom log farm is a positive loop setup ;)
**Dehydrators** tip - get the crystalarium from the 25k bundle and put a fire quartz in it, it will duplicate one every 24h allowing you to quickly ramp dehydrators.
**Green raid mossy seeds** - green rain will give you few seeds. You can plant them on your farm in-between your pine trees and make them grow moss even faster. Just replant the seed if you get the green-ish fern tree when planted.
**Fun names**: Trufflestein, Shroomster, Toadstool...

You've put the tapper on a Hardwood tree, which doesn't give Resin. if you put the tapper on another tree- oak, pine, or maple- it'll produce results.
If you're after RESIN specifically, you'll need to tap an Oak tree. I've added a photo of what they look like 😌

Well, shit, I had no idea you *could* tap hardwood trees. Do they always give that much sap? I'm always low on sap!

Different trees produce different things. The hardwood mahogany tree produces sap every day when tapped. Pine trees produce pine tar every 5 days. Oak trees produce oak resin every 7 days. Maple trees produce maple syrup every 9 days.

I got so confused cause i keep getting sap for like 4 days straight and i was used with just waiting for days and then getting resin. It's my first time seeing sap in tapper for this tree. Thank you very much
In the fall or after the green rain event, certain trees can turn into wild trees, which produce sap in tappers. It will turn back into a regular tree after the fall is over.
Here ya go!!

While we on subject. This might be a stupid question and english is not my first language.
Why is oak resin named resin yet Pine produces...Tar. like. Arent they both resins?
Pine tar is a product processed from pine resin apparently, i think it's just the game's way of giving us a variety of resources
Similarly, you don't get syrup directly from a maple tree, you make it from maple sap.

I think it’s to keep the item names more obviously different. Maple trees give syrup, oak give resin, pine give tar, and mahogany give sap.
It’s worth noting too that pine tar is a thing, but it’s not produced by tapping the tree, at least in our world
That’s a Hardwood tree, they produce sap

item.post_title: Fresh start optimized for mushroom logs instead of crops

children_1.comment_text:
**Overall plan looks like so:**
* upgrade axe to copper ASAP, even before pickaxe
* use the chair to go into secret woods every day (and/or use hardwood stumps on Forest and 4-corners farm)
* gather hardwood and plant mahogany seeds on your farm with a bit extra spread to them with clear ground around them
* let the mahogany grow and start dropping additional mahogany seeds
You want 300+ hardwood going into summer and 10-20 mahogany trees growing. Level 5 forester instead of gatherer can be better here as well.
For wild tree seeds:
* upgrade axe to steel
  + cut down every tree in the forest, mountains
  + cut down non-pine trees on your farm
* collect pine cones dropped by pine tree on your farm
* early summer start full-clearing your farm and start planting a grid of wild trees (you want a lot of pines for mushroom logs)
* cut down any regrown trees before green rain, after green rain and later on as needed.
**Tree fertilizer** can be handy on mahogany trees, but when used on any tree it makes them grow moss quicker as well, so bonus points for using tree fertilizer on every pine tree you plant.
**Green rain** will hit somewhere in summer and your goal is to purely go for moss - farm every weed and moss in the forest and open areas of your farm and other zones. You should get bit over 300 moss or 400 if you are on beach farm and it's mostly cleared before rain.
300 moss then gives you 30 logs which can produce around 1050 mushrooms per season. After green rain moss harvesting will be slow, but the more trees you will have the more moss you will be able to farm regularly so a mushroom log farm is a positive loop setup ;)
**Dehydrators** tip - get the crystalarium from the 25k bundle and put a fire quartz in it, it will duplicate one every 24h allowing you to quickly ramp dehydrators.
**Green raid mossy seeds** - green rain will give you few seeds. You can plant them on your farm in-between your pine trees and make them grow moss even faster. Just replant the seed if you get the green-ish fern tree when planted.
**Fun names**: Trufflestein, Shroomster, Toadstool...

item.post_title: Drew sandy a while back and was told to put it here too!
Why do i keep getting sap and not resin?

children_1.comment_text: i love oddly specific meme images
i like to think you waited at least a few years to whip this out. i am crying lmao
Ngl, I saw this on Tumblr YEARS ago and I've finally gotten a chance to use it lmao

"Give us oak resin and we'll burn your house to the ground"
"Uh, don't you mean 'or'?"
"Give us oak resin OOOOOR we'll burn your house to the ground"
You got me laughing at a mahogany joke in 2026, well done

You've put the tapper on a Hardwood tree, which doesn't give Resin. if you put the tapper on another tree- oak, pine, or maple- it'll produce results.
If you're after RESIN specifically, you'll need to tap an Oak tree. I've added a photo of what they look like 😌
remember hole at the top
That's how I remembered it too.
That sounds sus without context lol
Thank you so much 😭 now it all makes sense
Well, shit, I had no idea you *could* tap hardwood trees. Do they always give that much sap? I'm always low on sap!

Different trees produce different things. The hardwood mahogany tree produces sap every day when tapped. Pine trees produce pine tar every 5 days. Oak trees produce oak resin every 7 days. Maple trees produce maple syrup every 9 days.

I got so confused cause i keep getting sap for like 4 days straight and i was used with just waiting for days and then getting resin. It's my first time seeing sap in tapper for this tree. Thank you very much
In the fall or after the green rain event, certain trees can turn into wild trees, which produce sap in tappers. It will turn back into a regular tree after the fall is over.
Here ya go!!
Can…can I tap Elliot?…asking for a friend…

While we on subject. This might be a stupid question and english is not my first language.
Why is oak resin named resin yet Pine produces...Tar. like. Arent they both resins?
Pine tar is a product processed from pine resin apparently, i think it's just the game's way of giving us a variety of resources
Similarly, you don't get syrup directly from a maple tree, you make it from maple sap.

I think it’s to keep the item names more obviously different. Maple trees give syrup, oak give resin, pine give tar, and mahogany give sap.
It’s worth noting too that pine tar is a thing, but it’s not produced by tapping the tree, at least in our world
That’s a Hardwood tree, they produce sap
Wait hold on now, you can get sap this way????

item.post_title: I met CA as Penny at the Honolulu concert!

children_1.comment_text: How awesome! Do you mind sharing where you got your shoes from? They're so cute!
My shoes are from Dansko in the style Fawna. They’re the chestnut version that I dyed a darker brown
The ape isn't looking quite concerned

He should be less concerned after all the money he's made on the game.
No shade--he earned it by making something wholesome and great that has brought joy to a lot of people. He seems like a genuinely good dude.
